signal_reason:
2026-07-20 08:08:00

Market Last Trading Day (Friday) - U.S. equities finished lower during the last trading day as weakness in technology and semiconductor shares weighed on the broader market. The decline pushed the TReg Regime Signal below its confirmation threshold, resulting in a transition from RiskOn to RiskOff. Consistent with TurboRegime’s end-of-day methodology, the portfolio rebalance was executed near Friday’s market close after the signal was confirmed, rather than waiting until the next trading day or reacting prematurely to intraday volatility.

Market Today (Monday) - The market begins the week with TurboRegime positioned in the more defensive RiskOff regime. The allocation change was already executed near the close of the last trading day, so no additional trade is currently required. Today’s market action will help determine whether the weakening conditions that triggered the change continue or begin to stabilize. TurboRegime will remain disciplined and respond only to confirmed market conditions rather than attempting to predict the market’s next move.

Current Risk Regime - RiskOff is now in effect following the regime change detected on July 17. The previous RiskOn trade remained active for 12 trading days and closed with a return of -0.4% and a maximum drawdown of -0.4%. The new recommended allocation is 60% SPY, 40% SHV, and 0% SPXL. This change reflects a reduction in portfolio risk following the deterioration in the TReg Regime Signal.

Hold or Trade - HOLD. The RiskOff rebalance was completed near the close of the last trading day. No additional portfolio changes are currently indicated. The portfolio should now be allocated 60% to SPY, 40% to SHV, and 0% to SPXL. Importantly, RiskOff does not mean that TurboRegime has exited the stock market. The strategy remains 60% invested in the S&P 500 while increasing its allocation to short-term U.S. Treasury securities. It is a more defensive position, not an all-cash position.

Live Performance - Live tracking continues to demonstrate that TurboRegime can respond to changing market conditions while remaining closely aligned with its historical process. The recently completed RiskOn trade produced a modest loss of 0.4%, illustrating that not every trade is expected to be profitable. The objective is to participate when conditions are favorable and reduce exposure when the model detects increasing risk. Live execution continues to follow the same end-of-day confirmation and near-close trading methodology used by the strategy.

General Commentary - RiskOff is sometimes misunderstood as a prediction that the market is about to collapse or as an instruction to sell everything. Neither is correct. A RiskOff transition simply means that current conditions no longer support the higher equity allocation used during RiskOn. TurboRegime responds by reducing equity exposure from 85% to 60% and increasing the defensive SHV allocation from 15% to 40%. This allows the portfolio to remain meaningfully invested if the market recovers while providing additional protection if weakness continues. The goal is not to predict every market move—it is to adjust portfolio risk as conditions change
